notes:
The cabin slowstock shelf's unlock graph. Nodes are slowstick IDs; edges are 'finishing A unlocks B.' A stick becomes playable when ANY of its `unlocked_by` entries have been finished (OR-graph), or when it is in the initial `starts_unlocked` set. The shelf UI shows all sticks; locked ones render with a smaller, dimmer label and no boot option.

Progress persists to GauntletState.state.slowsticks_finished (a String[] of stick ids). A stick is 'finished' when its own resource fires the finish_slowstick event.

Sweetgum is technically starts_unlocked · its REAL gate is shelf visibility (hidden_until_token ps_1976_incident_known in shelf_layout). You cannot own this stick before you know why it exists.

waves.notes: Riffmaster is the family's own cart · it surfaces as soon as Tem starts digging.
Fey Faire and Earthman Chronicles both appear on Sam's slowstick console inside Pirate Summer.  Finishing Pirate Summer establishes their in-fiction discovery.  Boot from this shelf independently.
The camp cart · finishing Pirate Summer is how Tem learns what this stick IS. Olaf bought the Sweetgum console lot at auction in 2011.
RANCH's 2004 follow-up · surfaces once Tem has met RANCH through the official Sam.
Ono's half of the Ono–Rocha correspondence · it surfaces once Tem has met the Faire.
PDP Toys' physics arcade cart · surfaces once Tem knows PDP Toys made real games (finished the Riffmaster).

tideline_survey_2048.reason: Tem buys the stick in Vol 7 present and adds it to the shelf. Not there yet on earlier chapter loads.

shelf_positions_note: Physical arrangement on the shelf is in shelf_layout.json. Unlock order and shelf position are decoupled by design: the player who returns to the shelf mid-run always sees the sticks in the same physical arrangement, whether or not each is unlocked.
